title: Mushroom & Pearl Barley Risotto

overview.description: A creamy pearl barley risotto made with mushrooms, garlic and Parmesan. Inspired by northern Italian orzotto, it's a wholesome twist on classic risotto that's economical, comforting and packed with flavour.

ingredients: 1 litre hot chicken or vegetable stock
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
method: Heat the olive oil and butter in a large saucepan over a medium heat. Add the onion and cook for about 8 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and mushrooms and cook until the mushrooms have released their moisture and begun to brown, about 8–10 minutes.
Stir in the pearl barley and cook for 2 minutes, coating every grain in the butter and oil.
Pour in the white wine, if using, and cook until almost completely absorbed.
Add the hot stock one ladle at a time, stirring occasionally and allowing most of the liquid to be absorbed before adding the next ladle.
Continue until the barley is tender but still has a slight bite. This should take about 35–40 minutes.
Remove from the heat and stir through the Parmesan and parsley.
Season with salt and black pepper, cover and leave to rest for 2 minutes before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Pearl barley risotto is known as 'orzotto' in parts of northern Italy. It has a nuttier flavour than traditional risotto and is naturally higher in fibre.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How gradual stock additions create a creamy risotto, why mushrooms should be browned before adding liquid, and how resting the finished dish improves the texture.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A comforting, economical meal that introduces a classic Italian cooking technique while making excellent use of inexpensive pantry ingredients.
chapman-tips: Brown the mushrooms well before adding any liquid to maximise their flavour.
Keep the stock hot throughout cooking to maintain an even simmer.
Resist the urge to rush—the gradual addition of stock develops the creamy texture.
notes.batch-cooking: Best enjoyed fresh, but leftovers can be refrigerated for up to two days. It can also be shaped into arancini-style cakes and fried.
notes.leftovers: Loosen with a little stock when reheating, or turn into crispy risotto cakes.
